expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: canonical top-layer grouping without collapsing scenarios
expectations.evidence: In result.md under “Evidence-based Phase 4b contract coverage”, it states Phase 4b’s purpose is to group into the “canonical top-layer taxonomy” “without merging away scenario granularity”, and explicitly calls out an “Anti-compression rule” (e.g., “Do not merge distinct Workstation-only vs Library-gap scenarios when outcomes/risks differ.”).

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4b
expectations.evidence: result.md is titled “P4B-LAYERING-001 — Phase 4b … Contract Check” and includes a section “Alignment to primary phase under test: Phase 4b” asserting Phase 4b is the “canonical top-layer grouper” and defers few-shot cleanup to Phase 6, matching the Phase 4b focus.
